title: Validate beads/Jira state before returning commands
description: crew_format_completion returns shell commands like 'bd close ISSUE_KEY' without validating that the issue exists or the transition is valid. Add pre-validation: check issue exists, check current status allows the target transition. Warn if invalid instead of returning commands that will fail.

title: P2: Full activity feed for headless via hook injection
description: Because --settings injects hooks, hook server receives PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop events from headless processes identically to embedded PTYs. Add TerminalKind::Headless variant (no PtyHandles, no parser, no reader thread). Activity feed, cost view, and stats render identically for headless and interactive.

title: F-key Norton Commander-style status bar
/crew-stats CLI - workflow analytics text output
description: Aggregate data from completed tasks into trends: time per phase, cost trends, concern hit rates, mode distribution. Derive from existing state.json, interactions.jsonl, and cost records. Config: analytics.enabled, analytics.retention_days. Compare similar tasks across time. Show correlation between doc improvements and task speed. Effort: L. Files: new commands/crew-stats.md, state_tools.py (new tool workflow_get_analytics).
close_reason: Enhanced crew-stats.py with cost trends, concern hit rates, phase timing, recent tasks table. Added workflow_get_analytics MCP tool to state_tools.py + server.py. 820 tests pass.

title: Fix uninstall-opencode.sh: clean ~/.config/opencode/ paths
close_reason: Updated to clean both ~/.config/opencode/ and legacy ~/.opencode/ paths, MCP removal from global config

title: P1: Auto-headless for --no-checkpoints worktrees
description: When crew-board F4 creates a worktree with no-checkpoints enabled, auto-use headless mode. --no-checkpoints implies no human interaction, so requiring an interactive terminal is contradictory. Also default headless for quick mode (single-pass, no checkpoints).

title: v1.0.0: Team Features + Cross-Project
description: Release milestone: Multi-user and cross-project capabilities. Cross-project doc templates, team workflow sharing, workflow comparison/ROI tracking.

title: Add /crew-stats metrics dashboard
description: Cost tracking, agent performance, and error pattern data is collected but has no user-facing presentation. Add a /crew-stats command showing cost trends, agent precision, error hit rates, and mode distribution.

title: Richer Planner context output for downstream agents
description: Planner should save richer context that downstream agents can reuse instead of re-exploring the codebase. Two changes: (1) Planner includes relevant code snippets inline in the plan, not just file paths. (2) Planner saves a context-map.md listing every file explored and why. Implementer receives this and skips redundant exploration. Prompt-level change, minimal code. Effort: S. Files: agents/planner.md, orchestration_tools.py (_get_context_files to include context-map.md).

title: Cost tracking fails: Stop hook can't find completed task
description: The Stop hook (log-crew-interaction.py) fails to record session cost because crew_format_completion() completes the task before the Stop event fires. The hook's _find_session_task() skips completed tasks in both the .active_task check and the scan fallback. Result: zero cost data across all 86 tasks.

title: Copy workflow config to worktrees on creation
description: setup-worktree.py only copies .claude/settings.local.json to worktrees but not .claude/workflow-config.yaml. Untracked project-level config overrides are missing in worktrees. Need to copy or symlink workflow config for all platforms (.claude/, .copilot/, .gemini/) alongside existing settings copy step.
close_reason: Added workflow config copy step to setup-worktree.py after settings copy. Copies .claude/.copilot/.gemini workflow-config.yaml from main repo to worktree if present and not already there.

title: crew init: return full context + artifacts for instant resume
description:
Extend crew_orchestrator.py init to return all context needed for start/resume in one call: worktree detection, task artifacts, resume point, and assembled_prompt. Eliminates all LLM file-reading during /crew and /crew-resume startup. Script handles worktree vs main-repo transparently.

Phase-aware artifact optimization: only include artifacts relevant to the current phase. E.g. implementer gets planner.md but not ba_designer.md or product_manager.md. For verbose artifacts, extract only the relevant section (e.g. 'Implementation Plan' from planner.md). PM verdict is already in state.json — no need to include full product_manager.md downstream.

title: Make technical_writer phase conditional (skip when no docs needed)
Consolidate 69 MCP tools to ~30 via composite query/action tools
Add outcome tracking — task success rate, rework cycles, quality metrics
PoC: Multi-PTY memory and performance with 10-15 concurrent terminals
description: Spawn 10-15 concurrent PTYs with high-throughput output, measure memory usage and CPU impact on crew-board responsiveness. Test scrollback buffer limits. Go/no-go: memory < 500MB, no UI lag with all PTYs active. Can be done as extension of the TUI rendering PoC (AW-jv1).

title: Enhanced history view with full state.json details
P2: Add --tdd flag for test-first planning
description: Add a --tdd flag to /crew that restructures Planner output for test-first workflow: write tests -> verify red -> implement -> verify green. Loop mode infra exists, just needs a 'write tests first' phase. Naturally constrains scope (addresses over-engineering concern).

title: crew-board: visual stats dashboard (TUI)
Fix: orchestrator skips developer phase in turbo mode
description: workflow_initialize() hardcodes state.phase='architect' regardless of mode. For turbo mode (phases: developer/implementer/technical_writer), this creates a phantom architect checkpoint that confuses the LLM orchestrator, causing it to skip/misroute the developer phase. Two fixes needed: (1) crew_init_task should transition to correct first phase after workflow_set_mode, (2) workflow_transition should not auto-complete phases that never ran. See plan: ~/.claude/plans/structured-doodling-canyon.md
close_reason: Fixed: workflow_initialize now sets phase=None, crew_init_task transitions to correct first phase after mode is set. Standard mode (developer-first) no longer gets stuck on phantom architect phase.

title: PoC: Permission prompt detection in raw PTY ANSI stream
description: Test whether Claude Code permission prompts can be reliably detected via regex/pattern matching on raw PTY output (ANSI escape sequences). Capture raw PTY output from a real crew run, analyze patterns. Key concerns from security audit: (1) Claude Code uses rich TUI widgets not plain [Y/n], (2) False positives could trigger unsafe auto-approvals, (3) AI agent output could match permission patterns. Go/no-go: if prompts have unique detectable signatures that can't be spoofed by agent output, proceed with auto-detection. Otherwise, manual-only mode. See .tasks/TASK_094/security_auditor.md for security concerns.

title: v0.5.0: Speed + Friction Reduction
description: Release milestone: Reduce pipeline latency and eliminate host permission friction. Key themes: async Technical Writer, wider quick-mode detection, host-aware planner, permission allowlist generator, permission profile presets.
close_reason: All child tasks completed (AW-o9r, AW-ze1, AW-5m0)

title: Ship task templates for common patterns
description: The --task <file> pattern is underused. Ship 4-5 example task files (migration, refactor, new-endpoint, bug-fix, new-feature) in examples/tasks/ that users can reference or adapt with /crew --task.

title: Add GitHub Actions CI for tests and build validation
Add concurrent workflow guard per task
description: FileLock on state.json prevents data corruption but two simultaneous /crew commands on the same task are not guarded against. Add a task-level lockfile (beyond state.json) that prevents two orchestrators from running the same task simultaneously. Should warn the user if a workflow is already active for the task.

title: Extract shared agent preamble to reduce per-agent redundancy
Team workflow sharing
description: Bundle workflow-config.yaml overrides, permission profiles, and doc templates into a distributable team config package. New cascade level between global and project. Distribution via URL (fetch+cache) or checked-in file. Config: team.config_url or team.config_path, team.auto_update. Effort: M. Files: config_tools.py (new cascade level).

title: Host-aware Planner optimization
description: When running on Claude Code (or other hosts with native exploration), skip redundant Planner codebase exploration. Host already explored before /crew was invoked. Planner focuses on: reading knowledge base, checking directly referenced files, producing structured plan output. Config: host_aware.enabled, host_aware.skip_exploration per host, host_aware.planner_mode (plan_only|full). ~40% faster planning. Effort: M. Files: orchestration_tools.py (_build_phase_action), config_tools.py, agents/planner.md.
close_reason: Implemented host_aware config, planner_mode injection, plan_only mode in planner.md

title: Fix build-agents.py: OpenCode output to ~/.config/opencode/
close_reason: Added _opencode_base() to route global builds to ~/.config/opencode/ and project builds to .opencode/

title: Incremental Technical Writer doc updates
description: Track which source files changed (git diff --name-only) and only pass affected docs to Technical Writer. Cross-reference changed file paths against knowledge base docs. Store doc_sections_map in state.json mapping source files to doc sections. Config: documentation.incremental (bool), documentation.change_detection.method (git_diff|full_scan). Effort: L. Files: orchestration_tools.py (_build_phase_action for TW), agents/technical-writer.md.
close_reason: Simplified incremental TW: added changed_files_command to TW phase context, strengthened implementer docs_needed emission, updated TW prompt for incremental focus. PM recommended SIMPLIFY - skipped doc_sections_map/git-diff-cross-ref. 818 tests pass.

title: Context excerpts: auto-resolve line-range references in prompts
description: Planner writes <excerpt path=file lines=N-M /> in context-map. Assembler resolves to actual code. Budget-capped at 50KB.

title: Droid: output slash commands to .factory/commands/
close_reason: Added .factory/commands/ output to build_droid(): 13 slash commands with proper Droid frontmatter (description + argument-hint)

title: Add architect phase to standard workflow mode
Add error pattern JSONL rotation
description: The .tasks/.error_patterns.jsonl file is append-only with no size limit or rotation. A loop that generates many errors could grow this indefinitely. Fix: cap at 500 entries with oldest-first eviction. File: mcp/agentic-workflow-server/ state_tools.py, workflow_record_error_pattern function.

title: P2: Create Reliability & Recovery documentation
description: Users describe features that already exist (state persistence, circuit breaker, health checks, model fallback, RESUME.md). Create a consolidated 'Reliability & Recovery' docs section so users can find all resilience features in one place instead of hunting across README sections.

title: Doc gap detection in Planner
description: Add instructions to Planner to flag undocumented code BEFORE implementation. For each file in the plan that lacks docs in knowledge_base, call workflow_mark_docs_needed(). Technical Writer then prioritizes those gaps. Infrastructure already exists (docs_needed in state.json, workflow_mark_docs_needed() tool). Just needs prompt instructions added. Effort: S. Files: agents/planner.md (add section), orchestration_tools.py (_build_phase_action for TW context).
close_reason: Planner doc gap detection implemented: expanded planner.md with proactive doc flagging section, added docs_needed to TW phase context in orchestration_tools.py, 3 tests added (817 total pass)

title: Extract orchestrator loop into a Python script
description: Move the ~200-line /crew orchestrator prompt into a deterministic Python script. The main loop (crew_parse_args → crew_init_task → loop(crew_get_next_phase → spawn agent → crew_parse_agent_output) → crew_format_completion) should be code, not natural language instructions for the LLM. MCP tools already exist — just need routing/sequencing extracted from the prompt. Enables unit testing of orchestration, eliminates LLM misinterpretation risk, faster execution.
close_reason: Already implemented — orchestration logic is in deterministic Python MCP tools (crew_parse_args, crew_init_task, crew_get_next_phase, crew_parse_agent_output, crew_format_completion). The prompt is just the thin caller. Remaining loop glue is inherently host-specific and can't be pure Python.

title: Sync PLATFORM_DIRS in setup-worktree.py and config_tools.py for OpenCode
close_reason: Added .config/opencode to PLATFORM_DIRS in config_tools.py, setup-worktree.py, and WORKFLOW_CONFIG_PATHS

title: Simplify to 3 workflow modes (quick/standard/thorough), remove aliases
Add KB listing timeout to prevent init stalling
description: workflow_set_kb_inventory uses rglob() on the knowledge base path with no timeout. If the KB path points at a huge directory tree, initialization stalls. Add a 10-second timeout that aborts listing and continues with partial results or empty inventory. File: mcp/agentic-workflow-server/ state_tools.py.

title: Convention file section filtering via ai_context_refs anchors
description: Support path#Section anchors in <ai_context_refs> to extract only relevant sections from large convention files (e.g. cross-platform.md#WSL-Specific Handling).

title: P3: Promote parallel workflows in Quick Start docs
description: Parallel worktree support (/crew-worktree, crew-board TUI) is mature but buried as 5th example in README. Move it earlier and more prominently in Quick Start to improve discoverability.

title: P0: Headless launch option in crew-board (with hook bridge)
description: Add HeadlessTerminal variant in crew-board. generate_hook_config() produces standalone settings.json for headless processes. Spawn claude -p --settings <path> --allowedTools ... as background child. Hook server receives identical events — no changes to hook_server.rs needed. Add headless toggle to F2 launch popup. Phase A of headless integration.

title: Smarter auto-detection using file scope analysis
description: Current mode auto-detection is keyword-based string matching. Enhance workflow_detect_mode to analyze actual file scope (git diff stats, directory count, cross-module changes) instead of just task description keywords.

title: Bug: /crew ask gets hijacked by stale resume detection
description: crew_orchestrator.py init: the ask action should bypass resume detection entirely. Currently /crew ask architect 'question' returns action=resume for TASK_096 instead of action=ask. Actions like ask, status, and config are not workflow starts and must never trigger resume logic.
close_reason: Fixed: ask/status/config/proceed actions now bypass resume detection entirely in cmd_init(). Moved action routing before error-triggered resume logic.

title: Workflow comparison and ROI tracking
description: Group similar tasks by keyword/description and compare metrics across time periods. Show: 'Refactor tasks are 28% faster this month because docs improved.' Correlation detection between doc additions and planner speedup. Proof-of-ROI for the Technical Writer flywheel. Effort: M. Files: extends /crew-stats command, state_tools.py.
close_reason: Added task comparison by type (feature/refactor/fix/docs/test) to crew-stats.py with --compare flag. Groups tasks by description keywords and shows avg cost, phases, concerns per type. Included in full dashboard and JSON output.

title: PoC: TUI-in-TUI rendering - spawn Claude Code in portable-pty + vt100 + ratatui
description: BINARY GATE for embedded terminals. Build a ~250-line Rust example (crew-board/examples/terminal_spike.rs) that: (1) Spawns claude CLI in portable-pty 0.9, (2) Feeds PTY output through vt100::Parser 0.16, (3) Renders vt100 screen state as ratatui widget with color/attribute mapping, (4) Handles resize (SIGWINCH), (5) Tests Claude Code permission dialogs, spinners, diffs. Go/no-go: if Claude Code TUI renders at visual fidelity >= 3/5, proceed. See .tasks/TASK_094/developer.md Part B for full spec.
close_reason: PoC approved by user. Claude Code TUI renders well through portable-pty + vt100 + ratatui. Binary gate passed - proceed with full implementation.

title: P1: Document phase_stuck action in crew.md
description: The phase_stuck action is returned by crew_get_next_phase but is not documented in the crew.md action loop alongside agent_blocked and agent_escalated. Users and the LLM orchestrator don't know how to handle it properly.

title: Add security-specific tests for MCP tools
description: Current 432 tests cover functionality well but lack security-specific test cases. Add tests for: path traversal attempts in task_id, shell injection in branch names and worktree paths, oversized input handling (huge task descriptions, massive error messages), symlink escape in KB listing. Validates existing safety properties and catches regressions.
close_reason: Added 43 security tests covering path traversal, shell injection, oversized inputs, symlink escape, input validation, error pattern integrity, parse args safety, and concurrent access. Fixed real vulnerabilities: path traversal in find_task_dir (added _is_safe_task_id guard), shell injection in worktree git commands (added shlex.quote), and unquoted cleanup script args. 611 tests total passing.

title: Cross-project doc templates
description: Export/import docs/ai-context/ structure across projects. /crew-docs-export strips project-specific content, keeps section headers. /crew-docs-import creates placeholder docs that TW fills during first workflow. Templates stored in ~/.claude/doc-templates/. Effort: L. Files: new commands/crew-docs-export.md, crew-docs-import.md.
close_reason: New /crew-docs-export and /crew-docs-import commands with scripts. Export strips project-specific content, keeping structure. Import creates placeholder docs for TW to fill. Templates stored in ~/.claude/doc-templates/.

title: P2: Planner generates mandatory Out of Scope section
description: The Planner agent should generate an explicit 'Out of Scope' section in every plan to set clear boundaries. Currently this section is optional in task file templates. Minor prompt enhancement to agents/planner.md.

title: Fix install-opencode.sh: MCP registered to global config
close_reason: Changed MCP registration from project-level opencode.json to global ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json

title: Esc should never quit the application
/crew-permissions allowlist generator
description: Auto-detect project tools (npm, pytest, cargo, eslint, tsc) and generate .claude/settings.local.json allowlist to eliminate repeated 'May I run X?' host permission prompts. New command: /crew-permissions. Detects package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, Makefile, tsconfig.json etc. Generates Bash(command:*) patterns. Merges into existing settings without clobbering. Effort: S. Files: new commands/crew-permissions.md.

title: Async Technical Writer (Turbo Standard mode)
description: In standard mode, run Technical Writer in background after implementation completes. User can commit immediately, docs come as a follow-up commit. Config: documentation.async_mode (bool), documentation.auto_commit_docs (bool), documentation.notify_on_complete (bool). New action: complete_with_async_docs. Effort: M. Files: orchestration_tools.py (phase routing), crew_orchestrator.py (new action), commands/crew.md (action handler), config_tools.py (new defaults).
close_reason: Implemented async_mode config, complete_with_async_docs action, orchestrator handling

title: Section-filtered agent outputs: pass only relevant sections downstream
description: When passing planner.md to implementer, include only Implementation Plan/Assertions/Conventions sections, not full analysis narrative. Cuts ~60% of context bloat.

title: Support custom workflow modes from config in orchestrator
description: workflow_set_mode() only knows hardcoded WORKFLOW_MODES dict, ignoring workflow_modes.modes from workflow-config.yaml. _can_transition() rejects agents not in hardcoded PHASE_ORDER. Need to: 1) Read custom modes from config via config_get_effective, 2) Allow _can_transition to validate against mode phases, 3) Make agent prompt resolution fully dynamic.
close_reason: Added _resolve_mode() and _get_all_mode_names() helpers to read custom modes from workflow-config.yaml. Updated _can_transition() to accept custom phases from mode's phase list. Updated workflow_set_mode() to resolve modes from config. Updated _build_phase_action() to read max_turns from config.

title: /crew learn - standalone Technical Writer command
description: Run ONLY the Technical Writer agent against recent changes without the full pipeline. Makes the highest-value agent available independently. Use case: after making changes outside crew, run /crew learn to update docs/ai-context/. Runs against git diff, updates knowledge base. No workflow state needed. Effort: M. Files: new commands/crew-learn.md, orchestration_tools.py (new action path).
close_reason: Implemented /crew-learn command, crew_parse_args learn action, cmd_learn in orchestrator

title: Extract crew.md orchestration into Python/MCP code
description: The crew.md orchestrator prompt is ~600 lines doing argument parsing, state management, agent spawning, checkpoint logic, error handling, and beads integration. Extract into Python/MCP tools for testability, debuggability, and lower context cost.
close_reason: Same as AW-fgj — orchestration logic already lives in deterministic MCP tools (crew_parse_args, crew_init_task, crew_get_next_phase, crew_parse_agent_output, crew_format_completion). The prompt is just the thin caller. Closed as duplicate/done.

title: crew-board: Embedded terminal multiplexer (full implementation)
description: After PoC spikes validate feasibility, implement the full embedded terminal feature in crew-board. Includes: View 5 Terminals, PTY management, vt100→ratatui rendering, prefix key input routing, attention badges, permission detection, tiled layouts. See .tasks/TASK_094/ for complete design docs from BA Designer, PM, Developer, Reviewer, and Security Auditor.
close_reason: Embedded terminal multiplexer fully implemented: terminal manager, PTY spawning, vt100 rendering widget, terminal view, Ctrl+B input routing, attention system. All builds/tests/clippy pass.

title: P3: Auto-PR creation on worktree completion
description: After crew workflow completes in a worktree and changes are committed, optionally auto-create a PR via gh pr create. Add auto_pr config option under worktree section. 80% of infra exists (worktree lifecycle hooks, completion flow).

title: Merge workflow config into single file with stable/experimental sections
Expand agent parallelism across all crew phases
Trim implementer prompt — extract loop mode to separate reference doc
/crew-docs-report - doc quality metrics
description: New command showing knowledge base health: coverage (% of modules documented), freshness (days since last update per doc), gap count (unfilled docs_needed), trends over time. Store metrics snapshots in .tasks/_doc_metrics.jsonl after each TW run. New MCP tool: workflow_get_doc_metrics(). Makes the Technical Writer flywheel visible and measurable. Effort: M. Files: new commands/crew-docs-report.md, state_tools.py (new tool).
close_reason: New /crew-docs-report command: script (crew-docs-report.py) showing KB health, freshness, gaps. New workflow_get_doc_metrics MCP tool. 822 tests (1 pre-existing flaky timeout).

title: Permission profile presets (strict/standard/autonomous)
description: Single config key (permission_profile: strict|standard|autonomous) that configures both crew checkpoints AND host permissions together. Strict: all checkpoints, minimal auto-actions. Standard: planning+commit checkpoints, normal auto-actions. Autonomous: only before_commit checkpoint, all auto-actions except git_push. /crew-config --profile command for persistent change. --profile flag on /crew for one-time override. Effort: M. Files: config_tools.py (presets), commands/crew-config.md, config/worktree-permissions.json.
close_reason: Implemented permission_profile config key with strict/standard/autonomous presets. 814 tests pass.

title: Merge feedback phase into quality_guard
v0.6.0: Technical Writer Enhancement + Analytics
description: Release milestone: Elevate Technical Writer as the crown jewel. Add standalone /crew learn command, incremental doc updates, doc quality metrics, doc gap detection in Planner, workflow analytics dashboard.
close_reason: All child tasks completed (crew learn, doc metrics, analytics)

title: Fix install-copilot.sh: add MCP registration
close_reason: Already implemented - install-copilot.sh already registers MCP to ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json (lines 94-124)

title: PoC: Input routing with prefix key (Ctrl+B) in ratatui + embedded PTY
description: Build a small ratatui app with one embedded PTY and the three-state mode machine (Dashboard → Terminal Nav → Terminal Focus). Test with interactive programs (vim, htop, claude). Verify: (1) Prefix key (Ctrl+B) reliably switches modes, (2) No accidental keystrokes reach wrong target, (3) Visual mode indicator is clear, (4) Edge cases: prefix key + timeout, prefix while popup open. See .tasks/TASK_094/ba_designer.md section 3 for UX spec and .tasks/TASK_094/reviewer.md for input routing concerns.

title: P1: Fix first-phase failure detection (spawn_attempts=0)
description: When session dies before circuit breaker fires (first phase, spawn_attempts=0), nothing catches the failure. Add diagnostic metadata to crew_get_next_phase() for first-phase failures. Data: 19/99 tasks stuck at first phase with 0 spawn attempts.

title: P2: Expand quick-mode auto-detection keywords
description: Current auto-detection is too narrow. 'Rate limiting' routes to thorough mode because it contains 'API' keyword. Common real tasks like 'add a config option' or 'implement a utility function' should route to standard/quick mode. Review and expand the keyword matching rules in workflow-config.yaml.

title: Context summarization between agent phases
description: Use Haiku to compress prior agent outputs before passing to next agent. Needs quality evaluation framework, token threshold config, structured output preservation. See TASK_103 PM/BA analysis.

title: LLM-based mode auto-detection using Haiku
description: Add Haiku classification call before mode selection for smarter auto-detection. Requires fallback to keyword matching, platform abstraction for non-Claude hosts, and accuracy benchmarks. See TASK_103 PM/BA analysis.

title: Make checkpoint invocation deterministic and config-driven
description: Checkpoint presentation is currently interpreted by the LLM from the orchestrator prompt. Move to deterministic code: if config.checkpoints[after_{phase}] is true AND concerns exist above threshold, present checkpoint. Ensures human review is never accidentally skipped due to LLM misinterpretation. Ties into action item #1 (orchestrator script).
close_reason: Checkpoint invocation is now fully deterministic: _should_trigger_checkpoint() uses concern_threshold + concern_severity_threshold from config. _build_checkpoint_result() returns pre-built question/options so the orchestrator just passes through to AskUserQuestion. Default threshold=0 preserves existing behavior. 7 new tests, 618 total passing.

title: Expand quick-mode auto-detection keywords
description: Widen the keyword list and add regex patterns for quick-mode auto-detection. Add: fix test, fix lint, fix build, bump version, add/remove/update dependency, update config, toggle flag, change constant, fix formatting, delete unused, trivial, one-line. Add patterns: 'fix broken test', 'change X from Y to Z', 'set X to Y'. Raise max_files_affected from 1 to 3. Effort: S. Files: state_tools.py (workflow_detect_mode), config/workflow-config.yaml.
